claim_text: Prior human-subject DMI studies at higher field strengths have used multi-coil arrays37,44,45.

sub_claims.text: De Feyter et al. 2018 performed human-subject DMI at a field strength higher than 3 T.

evidence.snippet: Human studies were performed on a 4 T Magnex magnet (Magnex Scientific Ltd.) interfaced to a Bruker Avance III HD spectrometer running on ParaVision 6 (Bruker Instruments). The system was equipped with 67cm-diameter Magnex gradients capable of switching 30 mT/m in 1.1 ms.
The 2H spectrum acquired from the human brain at a lower magnetic field strength (4 T versus 11.7 T) has an inherently lower spectral resolution, resulting in partially overlapping peaks.
2H NMR spectrum acquired from human brain at 4 T, 60 min after oral administration of [6,6′-2H2]glucose.

sub_claims.claim_value: higher field strengths (relative to the manuscript's 3 T)
sub_claims.nuance: Defeyter2018's human-subject DMI was conducted on a 4 T magnet, which is higher than the manuscript's 3 T clinical platform. This satisfies 'higher field strength' framing.

sub_claims.text: De Feyter et al. 2018's human-subject DMI used a multi-coil / phased-array 2H receive coil.

evidence.snippet: Brain studies were conducted with a 28.5cm-diameter transverse electromagnetic (TEM) volume coil tuned to the proton frequency (170.5 MHz) for MRI and shimming. 2H RF reception at 26.2 MHz was achieved with a four-coil phased array that was driven as a single RF coil during RF transmission. The four 8 cm × 10 cm rectangular 2H array elements were positioned equidistantly on an 18 cm × 25 cm elliptical former that was positioned within the 1H TEM coil.
For human brain studies, subjects were positioned supine within the combined 1H TEM/2H phasedarray RF coil.

sub_claims.paper_value: four-coil phased array (for 2H reception, human brain)
sub_claims.nuance: The 2H receive chain for human brain DMI in defeyter2018 is explicitly a four-element phased array, driven as a single RF coil on transmit. This is a canonical example of a multi-coil array, matching the manuscript's framing. Note: the array is used only on the human brain studies; the human liver study used a single-coil surface probe.

attestation.indirect_attribution_check: The coil design in defeyter2018 references Adriany and Gruetter (ref 39) for the animal-brain integrated 1H/2H probe, but the human-brain four-coil phased-array is described as a primary implementation by the authors; citing defeyter2018 as a multi-coil-array human DMI example is a direct attribution.
attestation.out_of_context_check: Context matches: the manuscript refers to human-subject DMI at higher field strengths using multi-coil arrays; defeyter2018's human DMI at 4 T with a four-coil phased array is a representative instance.
